Title: Johor voters in Singapore frustrated by lack of leave to vote
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Several Johor voters working in Singapore have expressed disappointment over their inability to cast their ballots in the state election today after failing to secure leave from their employers.
Resmond Wong, 45, a store worker, said he was only allowed to leave early if he wanted to vote, but felt the concession was insufficient as his polling centre was located outside the city area.
"I know it is my responsibility, but we are not allowed to take leave due to a shortage of staff. The travel time back to Johor Baru also takes time, especially if there is traffic congestion," he said when contacted, adding that his family members in Pontian were expected to vote after midday.
Meanwhile, lab assistant V. Rabindran, 35, said he had missed voting several times throughout his employment in Singapore due to leave restrictions.
He urged the Singapore government to provide flexibility for Malaysian workers to return home for elections.
"Perhaps they can impose certain conditions so that we can return to Johor to vote," he said.
Another voter, Azizul Rahman, 30, said he plans to apply for annual leave much earlier for future elections.
"I am unable to return to Johor for this election, but for the next general election, I plan to apply for leave earlier. Some of my friends' employers encourage them to return, but others do not," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Iran hits back at Trump after insists truce over
Article:
TEHERAN: Iran insisted on Saturday it had "kept its word" on a ceasefire with the United States, after President Donald Trump insisted the truce was over but that he had agreed to further negotiations with the Islamic republic.
The comments mark a new low in relations between the foes, after an exchange of fire this week rocked a shaky agreement aimed at turning the months-long ceasefire into durable peace and threatened a return to full-scale regional war.
Trump ramped up the rhetoric between the warring sides on Saturday, threatening in a Truth Social post to "completely decimate" Iran if it attempted or succeeded in assassinating him.
It came a day after the president agreed to further negotiations with Iran, even as he repeated his assertion that the ceasefire was over, just weeks after Washington and Teheran signed a memorandum of understanding pushing for peace.
While there have been no direct talks between Iran and the United States since last month, Iranian media reported that a delegation from mediator Qatar was in Teheran after the sides exchanged strikes.
"The Islamic Republic of Iran has asked us to continue 'talks.' We have agreed to do so, but the United States has stated to them, in no uncertain terms, that the Cease Fire is OVER!" Trump posted on his Truth Social platform.
Earlier this week, at a Nato summit, Trump also declared the ceasefire over, saying of Teheran: "It's just a waste of time dealing with them."
Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi hit back on Saturday, insisting that Teheran "has so far kept its word, unlike the so-called US Treasury Secretary who is violating Para 9 of the MoU."
That refers to part of the memorandum of understanding that Iran will "maintain the current status quo of its nuclear program" and the United States "will not impose any new sanctions, and will not deploy additional forces in the region", pending a final deal.
"That violation follows other violations and missteps by the United States. Reality check: There can only be mutual compliance," Araghchi added.
US and Iranian delegations have held one round of direct talks in Switzerland since the signature of their memorandum of understanding, as well as indirect negotiations in Qatar, but there has been no sign of diplomatic progress since.
HORMUZ DEADLINE
The hostile words came as Axios and Politico reported that Washington has given Teheran until Saturday to stop firing on commercial ships in the Strait of Hormuz and acknowledge that the waterway is open.
The strait, a vital shipping route for the world's oil, is a major source of contention between the United States and Iran.
Teheran effectively closed the strategic waterway in response to the war triggered by US-Israeli strikes on the city in late February.
The Islamic republic insists it must control Hormuz, which comprises Iranian and Omani territorial waters, and has expressed a desire to charge fees for ships that transit it.
It did not have such powers before the war, and while the strait comprises Omani and Iranian territorial waters, under international law the two cannot generally block passage or charge tolls.
The United States launched extensive strikes on Iran this week following attacks on ships in the strait, triggering a wave of reprisals against American bases in the Gulf.
The US Treasury Department also revoked a temporary sanctions waiver for Iranian oil, cancelling a license announced in June that had allowed Teheran to produce, sell and deliver crude oil and related products through Aug 21.
Araghchi will travel to Oman on Saturday for talks on the strait, the official IRNA news agency said.
Doha backed the continued diplomacy, with Iran's Tasnim agency reporting on Friday that a Qatari delegation was in Teheran to "try to reinforce Qatar's role as a mediator following events on Tuesday", when Doha condemned the Islamic republic's "unacceptable assault" on one of its LNG tankers.
Iran denied the accusation.
Pakistan Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif, who has also been mediating the conflict, held a call with Qatar's emir on Friday to discuss the recent escalation, Sharif's office said in a statement.
Sharif also spoke with Iran's President Masoud Pezeshkian, urging him to safeguard the "hard-earned peace", the premier said on X.
However, Iran's chief negotiator in talks with Washington, Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, struck a defiant tone.
"Ending the war is a priority for the countries of the world, but everyone must know that this confrontation will never end with Iran's surrender," the ISNA news agency reported him saying.
Iranians, he said, were "fully prepared to defend ourselves."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor election turnout reaches 1.13mil by 1pm
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Voter turnout in the Johor election reached 1,130,980 as of 1pm today.
The Election Commission (EC), in its hourly update, said 42.16 per cent of the more than 2.6 million eligible voters had cast their ballots.
Early voting on Tuesday recorded a 95 per cent turnout, involving 19,471 voters.
Voting is scheduled to continue until 6pm, following the end of the 14-day campaign period at midnight last night.
The EC has projected voter turnout in the Johor election at about 70 per cent, citing strong public interest during the campaign, which it said reflected voters' enthusiasm to cast their ballots.
In the 2022 Johor election, voter turnout was 54.9 per cent of the 2.6 million eligible voters, among the lowest recorded in the country's electoral history.
The election was called after the dissolution of the state legislative assembly on June 1.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Fahmi, Ramakrishnan, Soo among Penang state honours recipients
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil was today conferred the Darjah Panglima Pangkuan Negeri (DPPN), which carries the title 'Datuk Seri', in conjunction with the 2026 Penang state awards, medals, and honours investiture ceremony, held to mark the 85th birthday celebration of Penang Yang di-Pertua Negeri, Tun Ramli Ngah Talib.
Another recipient of the Darjah Gemilang Pangkuan Negeri (DGPN), which also carries the title 'Datuk Seri', is Datuk Ramakrishnan Govindasamy, a professional engineer and industry figure with over 50 years of experience in mechanical engineering and the palm oil industry.
Among other notable recipients are Techninier Sdn Bhd chief executive officer, Datuk Peh Lai Yian—a corporate figure and entrepreneur with over 20 years of experience in the telecommunications, digital infrastructure, and utility digitalisation sectors; Datuk Soo Sze Ching, a corporate figure who has dedicated over 20 years of service; Mara director-general Datuk Zulfikri Osman; and Invest Penang CEO Datuk Loo Lee Lian.
The same ceremony also saw nine people receive the Darjah Yang Mulia Pangkuan Negeri (D.M.P.N.), while 72 received the Darjah Setia Pangkuan Negeri (D.S.P.N.), both of which carry the title 'Datuk'.
Among them were Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Ministry deputy secretary-general, Datuk Khalid Mohamed; former Penang police chief, Datuk Azizee Ismail; the late Nordin Ahmad, a renowned actor, director, and screenwriter for Cathay-Keris Film Productions; National Disaster Management Agency (Nadma) director-general, Meor Ismail Meor Akim; and Bank Rakyat chairman, Datuk Mohd Irwan Mohd Mubarak.
Overall, a total of 101 people received state honours, while another 1,120 received medals and honours this year.
The investiture ceremony will take place over four days, starting on July 11 and continuing on July 13, 14, and 15 at Dewan Sri Pinang.
The conferment of the Penang state awards, medals, and honours represents the state government's highest recognition for individuals who have made outstanding contributions in various fields, including public administration, economic development, investment, education, security, communications, and community service.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: BN's Md Jafni casts ballot, urges Johoreans to vote responsibly
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Barisan Nasional (BN) Bukit Permai candidate Datuk Md Jafni Md Shukor cast his vote with his wife and two children at Sekolah Kebangsaan Bandar Putra in Kulai today.
Despite heavy downpours today, about 34 per cent of voters had turned out at polling centres.
Jafni urged Johoreans to fulfil their civic responsibility calmly and responsibly.
After casting his ballot, Jafni thanked Election Commission (EC) officers, security personnel and voters for ensuring the polling process proceeded smoothly and in an orderly manner.
He said the sight of Malaysians from various racial backgrounds turning up to vote together reflected their shared aspiration for a better Johor.
Jafni also humbly sought the prayers, blessings and continued support of voters in Bukit Permai and Kulai as polling continued across the state.
He reminded those who had yet to cast their ballots to exercise their democratic right responsibly, while advising motorists to drive carefully and prioritise road safety when travelling to polling stations.
Jafni also expressed appreciation to supporters for their trust, prayers and unwavering support throughout the campaign.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Negri Sembilan polls: DAP determined to defend all 11 state seats
Article:
SEREMBAN: DAP is determined to defend all 11 state seats it holds in the 16th Negri Sembilan election on Aug 1.
Negri Sembilan DAP secretary Cha Kee Chin said the party would leverage its track record and public service performance to retain the seats.
"We cannot be complacent. Instead, we must continue to work hard to maintain a convincing victory," he said when contacted today.
Cha, who is also the Rasah member of parliament, said DAP would support its Pakatan Harapan allies to wrest the Paroi and Labu seats from Perikatan Nasional.
"Paroi has the highest number of voters in Negri Sembilan. The seat is expected to be contested by Amanah, while Labu will be contested by PKR," he said.
"We need to wrest the Paroi and Labu state seats. If we succeed, PH will be able to form the state government on the night of Aug 1."
Cha urged the party machinery to remain committed and work as a team to ensure victory for all PH candidates.
"PH needs to win this state election to continue the state's development agenda and citizen welfare programmes," he said.
In the previous state election, PH formed the state government with Barisan Nasional after winning 31 seats collectively, with PH securing 17 seats and BN 14, while PN won five.
However, PH and BN will contest separately this time, with both coalitions reportedly contesting all 36 state seats.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PLKN 3.0: 250 female trainees report for national service
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A total of 250 female trainees from across the country reported for the National Service Training Programme (PLKN) 3.0 (Series 3/2026) at the 515 Territorial Army (AW) Camp here today.
National Service Training Department director (Planning and Policy Division) Colonel Abd Rani Abdul Majid said 12 other trainees were granted special permission to report tomorrow to allow them to vote in the Johor election today.
"Today's registration mainly involves participants from the central zone, including Melaka and Negri Sembilan, while also welcoming trainees from Sabah and Sarawak," he said after inspecting the registration process at the camp.
PLKN 3.0 adopts a comprehensive training approach comprising 70 per cent military training and 30 per cent nation-building modules aimed at developing character, discipline, and identity in the younger generation.
Trainees and their family members arrived as early as 8am, with many expressing enthusiasm for the programme and its 45-day training module.
Wan Aminuddin Wan Ismail, whose 18-year-old daughter Wan Rabiatul Adawiyah is participating, described PLKN as a valuable opportunity despite it being her first time living away from home.
"I see PLKN as an excellent platform, especially in educating young people about safeguarding national security and instilling the spirit of volunteerism," he said.
Auni Alisya Safwana Safaruddin, 18, from Sandakan, Sabah, who enrolled voluntarily, said she looked forward to the activities despite feeling nervous about being far from home.
"This is my first time travelling this far from home. I'm a little nervous, but I hope to learn about new cultures and experience the military lifestyle," she said.
Another trainee, Esther Hiew Ru Xuan, 18, from Semenyih, Selangor, said she hoped the programme would build her character before she pursued higher education, adding that she was ready to take on the military-style challenges to become more disciplined and mentally resilient.
Registration for PLKN 3.0 was held simultaneously at two other training centres: the women's camp at Kem 504 AW in Bukit Keteri, Perlis, and the men's camp at Kem 505 AW in Pekan, Pahang.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 2 Indonesians held over alleged illegal fishing in Selangor waters
Article:
PORT KLANG: A fibre boat with two Indonesians suspected of carrying out illegal fishing activities was detained by the Selangor Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) in Kapar waters during an enforcement operation last night.
Selangor MMEA director Maritime Captain Abdul Muhaimin Muhammad Salleh said the boat was detained at about 10.30pm by the agency's patrol vessel 0.8 nautical miles southwest of Sungai Sementa Besar, Kapar, during routine patrols under an enforcement operation in Selangor waters.
"The fibre boat was intercepted after it was spotted moving suspiciously, before MMEA enforcement personnel carried out an inspection.
"Checks found the boat was operated by a 32-year-old skipper and a 23-year-old crew member, both Indonesians," he said in a statement.
Muhaimin said further checks found that both men failed to produce valid identification documents. The boat skipper also failed to produce any licence or permit authorising fishing activities.
He said the two men, along with the fibre boat and its catch, were escorted to the marine police jetty in Pulau Indah before being handed over to MMEA investigating officers for further action.
"The case is being investigated under the Fisheries Act 1985 for allegedly carrying out fishing activities without a licence or permission from the director-general of Fisheries Malaysia.
"Both suspects are also being investigated under the Immigration Act 1959/63 for failing to produce valid identification documents," he said.
Muhaimin said the MMEA would continue to intensify patrols and enforcement efforts to curb illegal fishing activities that could threaten the sustainability of marine resources and affect the country's maritime sovereignty.
Members of the public are urged to channel information regarding suspicious activities at sea through the emergency line 999 or contact the Selangor Maritime Operations Centre at 013-660 6652.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor election: Journalists return home to cast ballots
Article:
KLUANG: For many journalists covering the Johor election, today was not only about reporting on democracy but also participating in it.
Despite being assigned to cover polling activities across the state, several reporters made time to return to their respective constituencies to fulfil their responsibility as voters.
Utusan Malaysia reporter Hanafi Zulkefli, 25, travelled from Simpang Renggam to Tangkak after completing his reporting assignment in the morning, before resuming election coverage until the announcement of results tonight.
Hanafi, who was stationed in Batu Pahat with his colleagues throughout the campaign period, said balancing work commitments and civic responsibility was challenging but something he was determined to fulfil.
"I had to return to Tangkak after completing my assignment. This morning, I was covering events in Simpang Renggam.
"It was challenging to work on polling day, but both responsibilities are important.
"My duty as a journalist is to provide information on the voting process and the importance of civic responsibility, while my duty as a citizen is to exercise my right to choose the government," he said after casting his ballot at SK Rawang in the Serom constituency.
Meanwhile, Harian Metro reporter Mohd Redzuan Muharam, 37, said he returned to Johor Baru by train the night before after completing his assignment to avoid traffic congestion.
He cast his vote at Sekolah Agama Taman Perling 2 at about 10am and described the process as smooth and efficient.
"The process went well. I did not have to wait long, and the polling centre was well organised, with many officers assisting voters, especially senior citizens and persons with disabilities," he said.
Redzuan said he was relieved and grateful for the opportunity to return home and fulfil his responsibility as a Johorean.
"I hope whoever is chosen as the elected representative will carry out the mandate responsibly, remain close to the people and work towards Johor's continued development."
Berita Harian reporter Muhammad Esa Abu Yamin, 35, said he returned to Johor on Friday after taking leave earlier to ensure he could cast his vote.
He arrived at his polling centre in the Senggarang constituency as early as 8am and described the voting process as smooth and orderly.
"I feel happy and excited because the election atmosphere in Johor this time has created a strong sense of enthusiasm among voters.
"I am relieved and grateful to be able to fulfil my responsibility as a Malaysian and, more importantly, as part of the Johor community."
Muhammad Esa said he hoped the election outcome would bring continued progress and prosperity to the state.
"Whatever the result, my hope is that Johor will continue to move forward while prioritising the welfare and interests of the people.
"For me, Johor will always be Johor."
Polling for the Johor election began at 8am today and will continue until 6pm, with results expected to be announced later tonight.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Smooth traffic at Johor checkpoints as voters head to polls
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Traffic flow at the Sultan Iskandar Building (BSI) and Sultan Abu Bakar Complex (KSAB) remains smooth as 2.6 million voters head to the polls for the 16th Johor state election today.
Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said maximum operations had been activated at both land entry points between Malaysia and Singapore to facilitate the return of voters.
Based on updates from the Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (MCBA), Saifuddin said special lanes, hybrid counters, and contra-flow lanes were deployed to manage the anticipated congestion.
"I received an update from the MCBA team that traffic flow and movement are smooth at BSI and KSAB this morning," he said in a post on X, which included a 29-second video of the conditions at the checkpoints.
The special lanes operated for 24 hours yesterday and will remain open until 6pm today.
A total of 172 candidates are vying for 56 state assembly seats, with 1,076 polling centres and 4,889 channels opened simultaneously at 8am.
The Election Commission has targeted a 70 per cent voter turnout, with full results expected by 10pm.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police receive 90 reports, open 25 investigation papers during Johor polls
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Police received 90 police reports and opened 25 investigation papers throughout the campaigning period for the 16th Johor election.
Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Mohd Khalid Ismail said the reports and investigation papers involved minor offences that did not affect public order and were still under investigation.
He said none of the cases involved any election candidates or leaders from any political parties.
"So far, we have received 90 police reports throughout the campaign period up to yesterday.
"We have opened 25 investigation papers, most of which involve minor offences that I believe will not cause concern or tension among voters or election personnel during this election.
"Most of the cases we are investigating involve acts of mischief, where certain individuals were investigated for removing, damaging or attempting to burn flags and other related materials," he said during a press conference after visiting the polling centre for the Johor Jaya state seat at SMK Taman Molek here today.
Also present were Bukit Aman Internal Security and Public Order Department director Datuk Seri Mohd Yusri Hassan Basri and Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad.
Khalid said the investigation papers were opened under several laws, including the Election Offences Act 1954, Communications and Multimedia Act 1998 and the Penal Code.
Meanwhile, Khalid said 14,000 policemen had been deployed to all 56 seats throughout the election period.
Khalid said the voting process across Johor had so far proceeded smoothly and remained under control.
At the same time, he reminded the public to lodge police reports if they had any complaints or dissatisfaction involving voters or election personnel.
More than 2.7 million eligible voters are expected to fulfil their responsibility as voters in the Johor  election today.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor election: Age and disability fail to stop Johor voters casting ballots
Article:
KOTA TINGGI: Losing his left leg to diabetes complications seven years ago did not stop Abd Karim Taha from making his way to the polling centre today to exercise his right as a voter in the Johor election.
The 64-year-old former lorry driver from Felda Sungai Sayong woke up as early as 5am, eager to cast his ballot and fulfil what he described as his responsibility as a Johorean.
Accompanied by his three children, Nor Alia Maisarah, Nor Kamalia Amira and Abdul Luqman Hakim, aged 23, 25 and 28 respectively, Abd Karim arrived at SK LKTP Sungai Sayong despite his mobility challenges.
"Last night, I could not sleep because I was so eager to vote today.
"Alhamdulillah, the voting process went smoothly. As soon as I got out of the car, Election Commission (EC) officers welcomed me and provided assistance," he said after casting his ballot.
Abd Karim, who lost his left leg in 2019 due to diabetes, said he always made an effort to vote during every election as he believed the people had the power to choose leaders who could look after their welfare.
"I am proud to be a Johorean. We can choose a party that truly wants to take care of us," he said.
However, Abd Karim became emotional when recalling former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak.
"I always pray for his release. That is my only hope.
"It is not because of the party, but I feel he has contributed a lot to Felda and the people. Even now, when I watch videos of him, I cry," he said.
Abd Karim also caught attention by wearing a Jalur Gemilang-patterned tanjak, which he described as a symbol of pride in his nation and state.
Meanwhile, for former primary school teacher Md Yunan Abdul Majid, 61, the opportunity to vote again after recovering from a stroke was a meaningful moment.
He said he suffered a stroke in 2021, leaving him bedridden for five months and affecting his ability to speak and move.
"During the 2022 Johor election, I was unable to fulfil my responsibility as a voter because I was still recovering.
"Now, Alhamdulillah, I have recovered and can vote again after undergoing continuous physiotherapy and follow-up treatment.
"I feel very happy and relieved that I am finally able to fulfil my responsibility as a voter," he said.
Md Yunan, who was accompanied by his son-in-law Afiq Aiman Rahmat, 27, also expressed his appreciation to EC officers who assisted him throughout the voting process today.
In this state election, the Pasir Raja state seat is seeing a three-cornered contest involving Barisan Nasional candidate Datuk Seri Dr Adham Baba, Pakatan Harapan candidate Mohd Fakharuddin Moslim and Perikatan Nasional candidate Yuhanita Yunan.
In the 2022 Johor election, Pasir Raja was won by BN candidate Rashidah Ismail with a majority of 4,888 votes after defeating PN candidate Intan Jawahir (4,493 votes), PH candidate Mohd Fakharuddin Moslim (1,976 votes) and Gerakan Tanah Air candidate Mohd Yusri Yusof (265 votes).
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor polls: Heavy rain fails to deter voters
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Heavy rain and thunderstorms across Johor since noon today failed to dampen the spirit of voters turning out for the 16th Johor state election.
Continuous morning rain affected several polling centres in Muar, including Sekolah Jenis Kebangsaan Cina Limbong and Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan Sri Muar.
Voters wearing raincoats and carrying umbrellas made their way to the centres, determined to fulfil their duty despite the unfavourable weather.
In Johor Baru, similar weather conditions at SMK Bandar Uda Utama also failed to deter voters, with polling remaining calm and orderly.
The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) forecast thunderstorms this afternoon in several areas, including Iskandar Puteri, Johor Baru, Kluang, Kota Tinggi, Kulai, Labis, Mersing, Pasir Gudang, Segamat and Simpang Renggam. The weather is expected to improve by tonight.
Election Commission (EC) chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun said rain affecting areas such as Muar, Kluang and Johor Baru was expected to slightly impact voter turnout this afternoon.
He said the commission anticipated some disruption to the turnout rate but still projected it could reach between 65 and 70 per cent.
More than 2.6 million registered voters are eligible to cast their ballots today to choose their representatives for 56 state assembly seats.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man killed, three injured after car rams parked motorcycle, tree stump
Article:
KUALA TERENGGANU: A 39-year-old driver was killed when his car lost control, rammed a parked motorcycle, and crashed into a tree stump before overturning at Jalan Mengabang Lekar in Kuala Nerus early today.
District police chief Assistant Commissioner Azli Mohd Noor said police received information at 3.55am regarding the crash involving a Proton Saga and a Honda EX5 motorcycle.
He said the Proton Saga was travelling from Merang, Setiu towards the driver's home in Kampung Maras, while the motorcycle was parked on the left shoulder of the road facing towards Mengabang Lekar from Kuala Terengganu town.
"The driver of the Proton Saga, Che Mohd Hquzai Ali, 39, from Kampung Maras, Batu Rakit, sustained severe injuries and was pronounced dead at the scene.
"The crash also left a 45-year-old front passenger with minor facial injuries, while a 28-year-old rear passenger escaped unhurt," he said in a statement today.
He added that an 18-year-old motorcyclist also sustained minor injuries in the incident.
The injured victims were taken to Sultanah Nur Zahirah Hospital (HSNZ) for further treatment.
The case is being investigated under Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bersatu still strong despite Perlis MB's resignation, says Muhyiddin
Article:
MUAR: Bersatu remains strong despite the resignation of Perlis Menteri Besar Abu Bakar Hamzah and several of  state leaders leaving the party, said its president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin.
Muhyiddin said that although some of the party's leaders had resigned, he did not want issues involving the matter to be discussed outside the party before he made any statement.
"He (Abu Bakar Hamzah) resigned as a member of the Supreme Council, but he is still a Bersatu member.
"However, I will obtain a full report on the actual situation. As for internal party matters, I will not discuss them much outside the party," he told reporters after casting his vote here today.
Muhyiddin and his wife, Puan Sri Noorainee Abdul Rahman, cast their ballots at SMK Sri Muar.
Meanwhile, Muhyiddin said Bersatu remained intact despite several leaders resigning and leaving the party.
"Insya-Allah. Bersatu remains as it is," he said.
It was reported that Abu Bakar confirmed his resignation as a Bersatu Supreme Council member to focus fully on the administration of the state government.
Besides Abu Bakar, Sena assemblyman and state executive council member Marzita Mansor also submitted her resignation letter last week.
A similar move was taken by Perlis Bersatu secretary and Senator Azahar Hassan, who announced his decision to leave the party and relinquish all his positions with immediate effect.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bride hurries from wedding to cast vote in Johor polls [WATCH]
Article:
KOTA TINGGI: Less than an hour after becoming a wife, Fifi Nur Khairin Tamrin, 24, hurried from her wedding reception to fulfil her civic duty by voting in the Johor election today.
Fifi drew attention when she arrived at the polling centre at Sekolah Kebangsaan LKTP Sungai Sayong wearing a white wedding gown, accompanied by her husband, Muhammad Arif Fahmi Nor Azli, 28.
The newlyweds had just completed their marriage solemnisation ceremony at about 9am before making their way to the polling centre, where they arrived at 10am.
Fifi said they had fixed their wedding date in August last year, never expecting it to coincide with polling day.
She said that after the solemnisation ceremony and a brief photography session, she headed straight to the polling station before returning to continue the celebration with family and guests.
"It was a little hectic because after the solemnisation we took some photographs before coming straight here to vote.
"The important thing is that, as citizens, we must fulfil our responsibility and choose the right leaders."
Although Fifi admitted feeling slightly embarrassed by the attention she received in her wedding attire, she described the experience as a lifelong memory.
"I was a little embarrassed because everyone was looking at me, but this is not something that happens every day and it has become a historic moment in my life.
"I will remember this day even when I am old. I am proud that I was able to fulfil my responsibility as a voter, even on my wedding day," she said.
She added that the reception, held at her family home, was arranged around polling hours, with some relatives and friends choosing to vote before attending the celebration.
In this year's Johor election, the Pasir Raja seat is seeing a three-cornered contest involving Barisan Nasional (BN) candidate Datuk Seri Dr Adham Baba, Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate Mohd Fakharuddin Moslim, and Perikatan Nasional (PN) candidate Yuhanita Yunan.
In the 2022 Johor state election, the Pasir Raja seat was won by BN's Rashidah Ismail, who secured a majority of 4,888 votes after defeating PN's Intan Jawahir (4,493 votes), PH's Mohd Fakharuddin Moslim (1,976 votes) and Gerakan Tanah Air candidate Mohd Yusri Yusof (265 votes).
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Store fire near city centre sends thick smoke billowing over Johor polls
Article:
JOHOR BARU: A fire at a construction materials store in Stulang Laut sent thick, dark smoke billowing near the city centre today, though it did not disrupt the ongoing 16th Johor election.
The Johor Fire and Rescue Department operations centre said it received an emergency call at 9.39am and dispatched teams from the Larkin and Tebrau fire stations to the scene near R&F Mall.
Firefighters arrived at 9.47am to find the blaze fully engulfing the 25-by-30-foot storage shed.
"The fire gutted about 85 per cent of the structure. Crews immediately began extinguishing efforts using two hose lines and managed to bring the flames under control. Firefighting operations are still ongoing," the department said in a statement.
Larkin fire station officer Badrul Abdul Rahman said at the scene that the thick smoke was caused by diesel stored inside the facility.
He said the storage facility belongs to a construction company and is used to store equipment, materials, and other facilities for ongoing construction work in the area.
No casualties or injuries were reported as the fire was contained to the storage building itself.
A total of 22 personnel were deployed for the operation, supported by three Fire Rescue Tender engines and one Emergency Medical Rescue Services unit.
The incident did not disrupt the voting process at any nearby polling centres, and the cause of the fire is still under investigation.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: AirBorneo to open Kuching-Singapore ticket sales, fares from RM399 one-way
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: AirBorneo Airways Sdn Bhd (AirBorneo) will open ticket sales for its first international route between Kuching and Singapore on July 13 with all-in one-way economy class fares from RM399.
The Sarawak-owned airline said the daily service would begin on July 22 and operate between Kuching International Airport and Singapore Changi Airport Terminal 4.
All-in one-way business class fares will start from RM871. The fares include airport taxes, fuel surcharges, checked-in baggage and seat selection.
AirBorneo chief executive officer Megat Ardian Aminuddin said the Kuching-Singapore service marked a significant step in the airline's growth as it expanded Sarawak's air connectivity beyond Malaysia.
"Singapore is a vital regional and international hub and this new route will create meaningful opportunities for trade, tourism and investment between Sarawak and the wider region," he said in a statement today.
Ticket sales for the Kuching-Singapore route will open at 10am on July 13 through AirBorneo's official website and mobile application.
Flight MY363 will depart Kuching at 6.50pm and arrive in Singapore at 8.25pm.
The return flight, MY364 will depart Singapore at 9.15pm and arrive in Kuching at 10.50pm.
The Kuching-Singapore route will be operated using Boeing B737-800 aircraft that AirBorneo has wet-leased for its new jet operations.
Business Times first reported on July 6 that AirBorneo had filed planned B737-800 flights from Kuching to Kuala Lumpur and Singapore in the OAG airline schedules database.
On July 7, Business Times reported citing sources that the airline was believed to have wet-leased two B737-800 aircraft from Batik Air for its initial jet operations.
AirBorneo is also scheduled to operate its inaugural commercial service from Kuala Lumpur to Kuching on July 20.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor voter turnout reaches 34.78pct by noon
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Voter turnout in the Johor election reached 933,115 as of noon today.
The Election Commission, in its hourly update, said 34.78 per cent of the more than 2.6 million eligible voters had voted.
Early voting on Tuesday recorded a 95 per cent turnout, involving 19,471 voters.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor election: 103-year-old Kastawi proves age is no barrier to voting
Article:
KOTA TINGGI: Being more than a century old did not stop Kastawi Esa from fulfilling his civic duty when he turned up at SK LKTP Sungai Sayong here to cast his ballot in the Johor election today.
Kastawi, 103, was among the earliest to prepare for the journey to the polling centre, eagerly waiting for the transport arranged to bring him there as he was determined to fulfil his responsibility as a voter.
"I am proud to be able to vote. I got ready early this morning and waited because I couldn't wait to cast my ballot.
"Alhamdulillah, everything went smoothly. The election officers were very helpful. I am grateful that I am still healthy and able to fulfil my responsibility as a voter," he said.
Kastawi said the secret to staying healthy  was simply eating regular meals without following any special diet or taking supplements.
"I eat as usual. Sometimes I order food and people deliver rice and dishes to me. Alhamdulillah, I have not had any health problems so far," he said.
Meanwhile, Felda Sungai Sayong village head Shaiful Bahri Sidek, 60, said the village administration had arranged transportation to ensure Kastawi was able to vote.
He said Kastawi's presence was not only an example of fulfilling his civic duty but also an inspiration to the community, especially younger voters.
"Today, we are fortunate to be with the oldest Felda voter in Johor. I believe his presence will inspire other voters to come out and vote.
"He is an icon in this community.
"If someone who is 103 years old is still willing to turn up and vote, I hope it will serve as an example and encourage young people not to miss the opportunity to fulfil their responsibility," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: First-time Johor voters want leaders who deliver, not just make promises
Article:
JOHOR BARU: First-time voters in the 16th Johor state election today said they were looking beyond party affiliations, placing greater emphasis on candidates' integrity, commitment and ability to deliver on their promises.
Many young voters who cast their ballots said they wanted elected representatives who could improve the lives of residents, remain accountable and produce tangible results, rather than make commitments during campaigning alone.
Agriculture diploma student Ahmad Irfan Harith Ahmad Izwan, 19, said a strong sense of civic responsibility prompted him to arrive early at the polling station to avoid traffic congestion.
The Universiti Putra Malaysia (UPM) Sarawak student said he did not want to miss the opportunity to vote and had carefully assessed the candidates' performance throughout the campaign period.
"I want an elected representative who can improve the lives of residents, keep their promises and deliver tangible results. During the campaign period, I saw candidates who demonstrated those qualities," he told Bernama at the Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan (SMK) Bandar Baru Uda polling centre for the Larkin state constituency.
Meanwhile, Jolin Tan Pei En, 20, said party affiliation was no longer the main consideration for many young voters, stressing that dedication and integrity in serving the people were far more important.
"Regardless of the party, what matters is that the leader is hardworking, committed and genuinely serves the people," said the online clothing entrepreneur after casting her vote at SMK Taman Molek for the Johor Jaya state constituency.
Sharing a similar sentiment, Filzah Maisara Mohd Fuad, 19, described her first voting experience as both exciting and meaningful after casting her ballot at the Taman Setia Indah Religious School polling centre.
The Culinary Diploma student at Kolej Universiti Yayasan Pelajaran Johor said she was thrilled to see her name on the electoral roll for the first time.
"I feel excited and hopeful. I hope the leader I voted for is trustworthy, sincere in serving the people and capable of driving Johor's continued progress," said the Puteri Wangsa voter.
A total of 1,076 polling centres comprising 4,889 voting streams opened simultaneously at 8am today, allowing Johor voters to elect representatives for the state's 56 seats in the state assembly.
Polling, involving more than 2.6 million registered voters, is scheduled to continue until 6pm following the end of the 14-day campaign period at midnight.
—Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Two teen boys held over vandalism spree that damaged 8 vehicles in Gerik
Article:
GERIK: Police have arrested two teenage boys, aged 13 and 14, over a string of vandalism cases that left eight vehicles damaged with estimated losses of RM10,000.
District police chief Superintendent Abdul Samad Othman said the boys were arrested early today, less than 24 hours after police received eight reports of smashed vehicle windows in Taman Sri Temenggor, Taman Meha and Taman Gerik.
He said the pair were suspected of damaging three vehicles in Taman Sri Temenggor yesterday.
"In the incident, eight vehicles of various types sustained damage, with estimated losses amounting to RM10,000.
"Investigations found that the suspects are believed to have used stones and bricks to smash the vehicle windows," he said in a statement today.
[[nid:1485915]]
The case is being investigated under Section 427 of the Penal Code, which provides for a jail term of between one and five years, or a fine, or both, upon conviction.
Abdul Samad said initial investigations revealed that both suspects admitted to committing the offences with two other accomplices who remain at large.
He added that police had also seized several items, including a motorcycle, helmets and clothing believed to have been worn during the incident, to assist in the investigation.
He also advised parents and guardians to closely monitor their children's movements and activities to prevent them from becoming involved in criminal activities that could jeopardise their future.
"Parents may also be subject to legal liability if their children are found guilty of committing crimes, playing truant from school, or engaging in acts that disrupt public order, such as bullying and vandalism, due to negligence in their care and supervision.
"Members of the public are urged to continue providing information to the police promptly to assist investigations and prevent further criminal incidents from occurring," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor election: 26.43 per cent voter turnout as of 11am
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Voter turnout in the Johor state election reached 709,092 as of 11am today.
The Election Commission, in its hourly update, said 26.43 per cent of the more than 2.7 million eligible voters had cast their ballots.
Early voting on Tuesday recorded a turnout of 95 per cent, involving 19,471 voters.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Penang TYT calls for expedited construction of new ceremonial hall at Seri Mutiara
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Yang di-Pertua Negeri Tun Ramli Ngah Talib hopes that efforts to construct a ceremonial hall (Dewan Istiadat) at the Seri Mutiara official residence will be expedited.
He said a proper ceremonial hall was crucial to ensure that state investiture ceremonies are conducted with appropriate decorum, tradition, and grandeur.
"It has been the practice of the Malay rulers and governors to hold investiture ceremonies in a ceremonial hall within the palace grounds or the official residence of the head of state as a symbol of sovereignty, continuity of tradition, and the dignity of the institution.
"Therefore, it is only fitting for Penang to have its own ceremonial hall as a landmark of the state's grandeur and a platform to preserve our heritage," he said.
Ramli said this in his speech at the state investiture ceremony held in conjunction with his 85th birthday celebration at Dewan Sri Pinang today.
He added that the proposal for the construction of the facility had been submitted to the federal government for further action, and he hoped it would receive due consideration.
"The construction of this ceremonial hall is important to enable the state government to carry out its responsibilities in upholding the sovereignty and dignity of Penang," he said.
[[nid:1485937]]
He also reminded the award recipients that the honours were not just accolades, but a significant trust that must be maintained responsibly. He urged them to leave a legacy defined by their service and contributions to the people, rather than just their titles.
"For all recipients of the state awards and medals, I hope this recognition will serve as a beacon to continue serving with sincerity and become an inspiration to others," he said.
Communications Minister Datuk Ahmad Fahmi Mohamed Fadzil led the list of recipients of the 2026 Penang state awards.
The unity government spokesman, who is also Lembah Pantai member of parliament, received the Darjah Panglima Pangkuan Negeri (DPPN), which carries the title Datuk Seri, making him the sole recipient of the award this year.
A total of 19 people were conferred the Darjah Gemilang Pangkuan Negeri (DGPN), which also carries the title Datuk Seri. Among them were Chief Judge of Malaya Datuk Seri Hashim Hamzah, Mara Incorporated Sdn Bhd chairman Datuk Abdul Halim Mohammad, and Pelita Samudra Pertama (M) Sdn Bhd managing director Tan Sri Murugan Doraisamy.
Nine people received the Darjah Mulia Pangkuan Negeri (DMPN), while 103 received the Darjah Setia Pangkuan Negeri (DSPN), both carrying the title Datuk. Among the DSPN recipients were former Penang police chief Datuk Azizee Ismail and UEM Group Bhd managing director Datuk Amran Hafiz Affifudin.
Meanwhile, another 72 people were awarded the Darjah Setia Pangkuan Negeri (DSPN), which also carries the title Datuk.
The recipients included the late veteran Malay film actor, director, and Cathay-Keris Film Productions scriptwriter Nordin Ahmad and Bank Rakyat chairman Datuk Mohd Irwan Mohd Mubarak.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Over 30 motorcycles involved in LPT crash that killed 4, injured 20
Article:
KUANTAN: At least 20 people were injured, and more than 30 motorcycles were involved in the fatal highway crash that killed four motorcyclists at Km 246.4 of the East Coast Highway (LPT) today.
District police chief Assistant Commissioner Ashari Abu Samah confirmed the
updated figures
following the incident, which occurred near the Jabor toll plaza exit.
"A total of 20 victims have been confirmed injured and are currently receiving treatment at Tengku Ampuan Afzan Hospital (HTAA) and the Sultan Ahmad Shah Hospital, International Islamic University Malaysia (IIUM). The accident involved more than 30 motorcycles," he said in a statement.
The update follows earlier reports that four motorcyclists were killed and several others injured when a convoy of Yamaha RXZ motorcycles and a car collided while heading towards Kemaman.
Three of the victims died at the scene. They were identified as Che Mohd Sufian Che Ghani, 40, Muhammad Hafiz Al Hakim Mazlan, 33, and Mohd Aizat Husni, 33.
A fourth victim, Muhammad Safawi Md Noh, 24, died while receiving treatment at HTAA.
The crash also left a 13-year-old passenger in the car with neck injuries, while the driver escaped unhurt.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bangsar loses a beloved gem as Restoran Berjaya to close after 45 years [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: For generations, a meal at Restoran Berjaya was more than just a dining experience — it was a familiar taste of home, a place of family gatherings and memories that spanned decades.
The beloved neighbourhood eatery for Bangsar residents, which has been serving customers for 45 years, has announced that it will soon close its doors, leaving longtime patrons sharing heartfelt memories of their favourite dishes and moments spent there.
In a statement, the family-run restaurant announced its closure on Facebook and said the decision was not an easy one, but rising rental costs and the realities of old age had led them to bring the restaurant's long journey to a close.
It said they had the privilege of seeing customers who first visited as children come back with their own families, children and even grandchildren.
"After 45 years of serving our community, it is with a heavy heart that we announce the closure of Restoran Berjaya.
"Our restaurant has been more than just a place to enjoy a meal — it has been a place where memories were made, reunions were celebrated, and countless special moments were shared," it said.
The announcement sparked an emotional response from patrons, many of whom shared personal stories of their favourite meals and cherished moments at the restaurant.
For some, Restoran Berjaya was a taste of home that stayed with them through different stages of life.
One patron, Liza Salim, said she often craved the restaurant's mihun curry after spending 2.5 years studying overseas.
"I grew up eating them almost every week. When I got home, I told my dad about that dream, and he immediately went out and bought it for me. The best meal I ever had!" she said.
Another customer, Intan Bazura Zakaria, said she still holds a thoughtful gesture from the restaurant more than three decades ago when she was pregnant with her first child.
Intan said she had travelled from Wangsa Maju with her husband because she was craving char kuey teow even though the dish was only available later in the afternoon.
She said the aunty at the restaurant noticed her baby bump and decided to prepare a plate specially for her.
"I'm deeply grateful for your kind gesture. Wishing all the best to you and your family," she said.
For many, the restaurant was also a place that connected generations.
Nick Wong said he first visited Restoran Berjaya as a child with his parents and later brought his own child there.
"Thank you for being part of my life for so long. I will miss your curry mee and dendeng. All the best to you, ma'am. I hope you enjoy your well-deserved retirement," he said.
Malaysian band Pop Shuvit's DJ Uno, who has been a patron since 2002, also expressed his sadness over the closure.
"Thank you for the good memories and, of course, the best wantan and nasi daging dingding in the world! Been going since 2002, daripada bujang till now two kids, aunty still remembers me like we never left," he said.
The restaurant also extended its gratitude to customers, past and present staff members, and everyone who had supported Restoran Berjaya throughout its 45-year journey.
Although the restaurant's doors will soon close, the family behind the operations said the memories created with customers would remain forever.
"It has been one of the greatest honours of our lives to serve you and your families," they said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: #NSTviral: World Cup banter: MAS joins Norway-England airline rivalry with cheeky social media post
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia Airlines has joined the FIFA World Cup fever with a cheeky social media response to an airline rivalry between Norwegian Air and British Airways.
The exchange began when Norwegian Air challenged British Airways to switch their Instagram logos if Norway beats England in the quarter-finals scheduled for 5am on Sunday (Malaysian time).
Tagging British Airways, Norwegian Air posted: "Hey, @british_airways, do you want to make a bet? If Norway wins, you will switch to our logo on Instagram on Sunday (one day). And vice versa. Deal?"
While British Airways eventually accepted the bet, Malaysia Airlines and other carriers joined the banter.
Malaysia's flag carrier wrote in a witty reply: "We'll be watching with our satay in one hand and signature drink in the other."
Swiss Air wrote: "No time for popcorn. We have to prepare for Lionel Messi," referring to Switzerland's quarter-final clash against Argentina scheduled for 9am tomorrow.
Meanwhile, Qatar Airways also joined the exchange, saying it would provide live updates from the pitch.
At the time of writing, the challenge posted by Norwegian Air on Thursday had attracted 155,000 likes, 5,413 comments, 4,190 reposts and 132,000 shares.
The reply by Malaysia Airlines had garnered 3,245 likes.
As the FIFA World Cup fever continues to heat up, the playful exchange shows how brands are increasingly quick to tap into the power of social media and the excitement surrounding football's biggest stage.
View this post on Instagram
A post shared by Norwegian (@flynorwegian)
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Father, son seriously injured in 10-vehicle pileup on AMJ Highway
Article:
MELAKA: A motorcyclist and his 11-year-old son were seriously injured in a 10-vehicle collision on the Alor Gajah-Melaka Tengah-Jasin (AMJ) Highway in Cheng last night.
State Traffic Enforcement and Investigation Department chief Superintendent Muhammad Zaki Ramat said the 9pm accident involved seven cars, a lorry, a four-wheel-drive vehicle, and the Honda Dash motorcycle carrying the father and son.
He said the 39-year-old motorcyclist suffered a broken leg, while his son sustained serious head injuries and required intubation after their motorcycle was struck from behind.
"Preliminary investigations found that a Toyota Hilux travelling from Malim towards Cheng is believed to have lost control before crashing into several vehicles that had stopped at a traffic light intersection, including the motorcycle carrying the two victims.
"As a result, the motorcyclist suffered a broken leg, while the pillion rider sustained head injuries and required intubation. The 29-year-old Toyota Hilux driver suffered minor facial injuries," he said in a statement today.
Zaki said all the injured victims were taken to Melaka Hospital for treatment.
He said drug and breathalyser tests conducted on the Toyota Hilux driver returned negative results. However, a blood sample was taken and will be sent to the Melaka Chemistry Department for toxicology analysis.
He added that all vehicles involved will be sent to the Computerised Vehicle Inspection Centre (Puspakom) in Alor Gajah for further inspection.
The case is being investigated under Section 43(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987.
Zaki also said dashcam footage of the incident had been obtained and would form part of the investigation.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor polls: PH's Nur Hafiz Roslan casts vote in former school, praises encouraging turnout
Article:
KLUANG: Pakatan Harapan candidate for Machap, Nur Hafiz Roslan, cast his ballot at Sekolah Kebangsaan Kampung Melayu here at 9.15am today.
Wearing a maroon shirt, Nur Hafiz arrived at the polling centre accompanied by his mother.
He expressed satisfaction with the encouraging voter turnout and said the atmosphere at the polling centre was positive, with many voters already arriving early to cast their ballots.
The Amanah Youth executive committee member also shared that the school held personal significance for him as he was a former pupil there when he was in Standard Two.
"Alhamdulillah, I have just completed voting. The turnout has been very encouraging.
"I hope this momentum continues and that more people will come out to vote," he said.
Earlier, Nur Hafiz said he was encouraged by the long queues and voter enthusiasm since early morning.
"Alhamdulillah, the turnout has been encouraging since early this morning. Many people are here, and the queues are long.
"Earlier, at channel four, I also had the opportunity to meet old friends. Many have come out to vote, and the enthusiasm among voters is very encouraging."
He also called on Johoreans to fulfil their responsibility as voters and prayed for a smooth election process.
"Insya-Allah, may Johor be protected by Allah."
Nur Hafiz is contesting the Machap state seat in a straight fight against caretaker Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi, who is defending the seat under the Barisan Nasional banner.
Polling for the Johor election began at 8am today and will continue until 6pm, with voters across all 56 state constituencies deciding the next state government.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor polls: MCMC receives 29 complaints over fake news, hate speech
Article:
KULAI: The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) received 29 complaints involving the spread of fake news, hate speech and fraud during the Johor election.
Deputy Communications Minister Teo Nie Ching said complaints were lodged between the dissolution of the Johor State Legislative Assembly on June 1 and during the election campaign on July 9.
She said that of the 29 complaints, 17 involved the spread of fake news, while 11 were related to hate speech and one involved an online fraud case.
Of the hate speech cases, nine complaints were related to racial issues, with one case each involving religion and the royal institution.
"Throughout the campaign period from June 1 to July 9, 2026, MCMC received 29 complaints related to fake news, hate speech and fraud that occurred during the campaign.
"Of that number, we have already requested that the relevant platforms take action against the reported content," she said.
She told reporters after casting her vote at SJK (C) Kulai Besar here today.
Teo, who is also Johor DAP chairman, arrived at the polling centre at 9.20am, completed voting at 9.30am and left at approximately 9.45am.
Also present was Pakatan Harapan candidate for the Bukit Permai state seat, Mohamad Shafwan Ani.
Elaborating further, she urged all voters to be more vigilant against the spread of unverified information and not to be easily swayed by content laced with hatred on social media.
"Today is the day the people of Johor make their choice. I urge all voters to verify every piece of information they receive and not to be easily influenced by fake news or hate-laden statements.
"Be a digitally literate and discerning voter," she said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: AirBorneo to open ticket sales for Kuching-Singapore flights ahead of July 22 launch
Article:
KUCHING, July 11 — AirBorneo Airways will begin selling tickets for its inaugural international service between Kuching and Singapore on July 13, ahead of the launch of daily flights on July 22, which coincides with the Hari Sarawak celebration.
In a statement today, the Sarawak-owned airline said ticket sales would open at 10 am on July 13, with introductory all-inclusive one-way fares starting from RM399 for Economy Class and RM871 for Business Class.
“The new daily service between Kuching International Airport and Singapore Changi Airport Terminal 4 will strengthen Sarawak’s connectivity with one of the region’s key aviation and business hubs, while opening new opportunities for trade, tourism and investment between Sarawak and Singapore,” the statement said.
AirBorneo chief executive officer Megat Ardian Wira Mohd Aminuddin described the launch as a key milestone in the airline’s development.
He said the Kuching-Singapore service marked a significant step in AirBorneo’s growth, extending Sarawak’s air connectivity beyond Malaysia’s borders for the first time.
“Singapore is a vital regional and international hub, and this new route will create meaningful opportunities for trade, tourism and investment between Sarawak and the wider region.
“We remain committed to connecting people, businesses and opportunities through safe and reliable air travel. This milestone reflects our long-term ambition to expand AirBorneo’s international network in support of Sarawak’s economic and social development,” he said in the statement.
The airline said the inaugural flight’s timing was especially significant as it coincides with Hari Sarawak on July 22, when the state commemorates the anniversary of attaining self-government and celebrates its continued progress.
“The launch of AirBorneo’s first international route on this occasion reflects Sarawak’s growing aviation ambitions, reinforcing the state’s confidence in expanding its connectivity, trade and opportunities beyond Malaysia’s shores,” it added.
Under the flight schedule, Flight MY363 will depart Kuching daily at 6.50 pm and arrive at Singapore Changi Airport Terminal 4 at 8.25 pm, while the return service, Flight MY364, will depart Singapore at 9.15 pm and arrive in Kuching at 10.50 pm.
Tickets are available exclusively through AirBorneo’s official website and mobile application.
AirBorneo, which transitioned from MASwings in January this year, currently serves 21 destinations with a fleet comprising two Boeing 737-800 aircraft, eight ATR 72-500 aircraft and six DHC-6-400 Twin Otter aircraft, connecting major urban centres and remote highland communities across Sarawak. — Bernama

Title: Sarawak Immigration crackdown around Kuching, Kota Samarahan nets 24 foreign nationals
Article:
KUCHING, July 11 — The Sarawak Immigration Department detained 24 foreign nationals during Op Minyak, Op Selera, and Op Mahir, which were carried out at seven locations around Kuching and Kota Samarahan yesterday.
In a statement issued today, the department said the six-hour operation, which began at 9 am, was conducted following public complaints and prior intelligence gathered by the authorities.
Inspections focused on heavy machinery workshops in Kampung Semeba and Kampung Sudat in Kuching, as well as eateries and premises suspected of conducting online gambling activities in Muara Tuang, Kota Samarahan.
Several scrap metal and second-hand goods premises around Kota Samarahan were also inspected to ensure compliance with the country’s immigration laws.
A total of 24 foreign nationals were detained on suspicion of committing offences under Section 6(1)© and Section 15(1)© of the Immigration Act 1959/63 (Act 155). In addition, one witness summons was issued to assist with further investigations into the case, the department said.
The department also urged the public to channel information regarding the presence of undocumented immigrants or any unlawful activities to the authorities for further action. — Bernama

Title: LPT crash: More than 30 motorcycles believed involved in illegal racing, says police
Article:
KUANTAN, July 11 — A total of 20 people injured in an accident at Km246.4 of the East Coast Expressway (LPT) early today received treatment at two hospitals here.
District police chief ACP Ashari Abu Samah said 18 victims were treated at Tengku Ampuan Afzan Hospital (HTAA), while two others received treatment at Sultan Ahmad Shah Medical Centre here.
“Further investigations revealed that more than 30 motorcycles, believed to have been racing, were involved in the accident that claimed four lives,” he said when contacted.
Earlier, Ashari said the crash, which occurred at about 1 am, claimed the lives of Che Mohd Sufian Che Ghani, 40, Muhammad Hafiz Al Hakim Mazlan, 33, and Mohd Aizat Husni, 33, who were all riding Yamaha RXZ motorcycles. They died at the scene.
He said another victim, Muhammad Safawi Md Noh, 24, died while receiving treatment at HTAA.
The accident is believed to have occurred when a motorcycle hit the rear of a car and fell onto the right lane, resulting in a chain-reaction collision involving several other motorcyclists.
Meanwhile, Che Mohd Sufian’s wife, Faridah Mat Asri, 39, said she was informed about the incident by a friend at about 1.30 am.
The mother of four said her husband and several friends had travelled to Kuantan from Terengganu, among others, to enjoy a meal at a popular traditional Pahang food restaurant in the city.
“The last time I saw him was yesterday afternoon, before I took our children to the beach. The last message I received from him was at midnight, and after that there were no more messages,” she told reporters when met at HTAA. — Bernama

Title: Johor polls: Voter turnout hits 42pc by 1pm, ahead of previous state election pace
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — More than 1.13 million voters, or 42.16 per cent of the electorate, had cast their ballots by 1pm today in the 16th Johor state election, according to the Election Commission (EC).
In a Facebook post, the EC said the Semarang and Sedili state constituencies recorded the highest voter turnout at 50.5 per cent each, followed by Tanjung Surat at 48.6 per cent.
Penggaram registered the lowest turnout at 32.2 per cent, followed by Bentayan at 32.6 per cent and Simpang Jeram at 35.2 per cent.
The turnout surpassed the pace recorded at the same point during the 15th Johor state election, when 888,605 voters, or 34.51 per cent of the electorate, had cast their ballots by 1pm.
Polling, involving more than 2.6 million registered voters across 56 state constituencies, continues until 6pm today.
Counting of votes will begin after polling closes, with results expected later tonight.

Title: Johor polls: IGP says no candidates implicated in 90 election-related police reports
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — Police received a total of 90 reports across Johor during the 14-day campaign period for the 16th Johor State Election, which ended at midnight yesterday.
Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Mohd Khalid Ismail said none of the reports involved election candidates or senior leaders of the contesting political parties.
Of the total, he said, 25 investigation papers had been opened to facilitate further action in accordance with the law.
He said most of the reports were related to acts of mischief, including the damaging, removal and burning of political party flags.
“At this stage, all the investigation papers are still being processed to identify those responsible,” he told reporters after visiting the polling centre at Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan Taman Molek today.
Also present to monitor the voting process were Internal Security and Public Order Department director Datuk Seri Mohd Yusri Hassan Basri and Johor Police Chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad.
Commenting on polling day, Mohd Khalid said the voting process involving more than 2.7 million registered voters had proceeded smoothly, with no untoward incidents reported so far.
To ensure public safety and maintain order, he said 14,000 police officers and personnel had been deployed across all 56 state constituencies in Johor.
“The deployment includes 5,600 officers and personnel from contingents nationwide who have been assigned to assist with election duties,” he said.
He also urged voters with complaints or grievances related to the election process to lodge reports with the police to enable the Election Commission and other relevant agencies to take prompt action.
Polling, involving more than 2.7 million registered voters, will continue until 6 p.m. today following the conclusion of the 14-day campaign period at midnight yesterday.
He also urged voters with complaints or grievances related to the election process to lodge reports with the police to enable the Election Commission and other relevant agencies to take prompt action.
Polling, involving more than 2.7 million registered voters, will continue until 6 pm today following the conclusion of the 14-day campaign period at midnight yesterday.
For the latest news on the 16th Johor State Election, visit https://prn.bernama.com/johor. — Bernama

Title: Heavy rain and thunderstorms lash Johor, but voters brave the weather to keep polling on track
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — Heavy rain and thunderstorms across Johor since midday today have not dampened the spirit of voters turning out for the 16th Johor state election.
A Bernama check at several polling centres in Muar found continuous rain since morning affecting Sekolah Jenis Kebangsaan (SJK) Cina Limbong and Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan (SMK) Sri Muar.
Some voters were seen wearing raincoats while others carried umbrellas as they made their way to polling centres, determined to fulfil their duty despite the unfavourable weather.
In Johor Bahru, similar weather conditions at SMK Bandar Uda Utama also failed to deter voters from playing their part, with polling centres remaining calm and orderly.
According to the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia), thunderstorms are forecast to hit several areas in Johor this afternoon, including Iskandar Puteri, Johor Bahru, Kluang, Kota Tinggi, Kulai, Labis, Mersing, Pasir Gudang, Segamat and Simpang Renggam.
However, the weather is expected to improve by tonight, with no rain forecast for most areas.
Earlier, Election Commission (EC) chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun told a press conference that rain affecting several areas, including Muar, Kluang and Johor Bahru, was expected to have a slight impact on voter turnout this afternoon.
Due to the rainy weather, the EC anticipates some disruption to the turnout rate, but still projects it could reach around 65 to 70 per cent.
Johor takes centre stage today as more than 2.6 million registered voters head to the polls to choose their representatives for 56 state assembly seats in the 16th Johor state election.
For the latest updates on the 16th Johor state election, please visit https://prn.bernama.com/johor. — Bernama

Title: Smooth traffic at Johor Causeway, Second Link and main JB roads on polling day
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 – Traffic conditions on several major routes in Johor were reported to be free-flowing to moderate on polling day despite an increase in the number of vehicles following the return of voters from Singapore.
Checks at the main south-bound routes at the Skudai toll plaza and Kulai toll plaza showed a smooth traffic flow towards the Johor Bahru city centre.
By late morning, no unusual congestion were reported despite the expected higher traffic volume earlier in the day.
The same situation was also recorded on the Johor Causeway, where vehicle movement from Singapore to Johor Bahru city at the Sultan Iskandar Building (BSI) was reported to be at moderate flow towards the afternoon.
Traffic flow from the republic was reported to be heavy at 8am, but gradually eased by late morning.
On the Second Link (LinKedua) Crossing, via the the Sultan Abu Bakar Complex (KSAB), traffic flow was reported at moderate flow with vehicles moving consistently without significant disruption.
Despite the increase in vehicles entering the Johor Bahru city centre, traffic was reported to be at a moderate flow towards the afternoon. July 11, 2026. — Picture by Ben Tan
Last Wednesday, the Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (AKPS) announced that their personnel will operate at maximum capacity at both the BSI and KSAB to facilitate the return of voters from Singapore for the Johor state election today.
Among the initiatives included opening dedicated lanes, activating hybrid counters and implementing contra-flow lanes to ensure smooth traffic flow and traveller movement at the country’s two main land entry points.
The advance planning was seen to assist in the smooth traffic flow at Johor’s main entry points, ensuring an efficient and orderly return journey to voters.
Previously, the Malaysian Highway Authority (LLM) expected more than 300,000 voters from outside of Johor to return to fulfill their responsibilities in this election.
The Johor state election will see 172 candidates contesting 56 state assembly seats, with polling scheduled until 6pm today.

Title: Johor polls: More than 709,000 voters cast ballots by 11am as turnout hits 26.43pc
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — More than 709,000 voters, or 26.43 per cent of registered electors, had cast their ballots as of 11am today in the 16th Johor state election, Election Commission (EC) chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun said.
Ramlan said the turnout figure was recorded after polling began at 8am across 1,076 polling centres involving 4,889 polling streams throughout the state.
The Johor state election sees more than 2.6 million registered voters casting their ballots to elect representatives for 56 State Legislative Assembly seats contested by 172 candidates.
Earlier, the EC recorded a voter turnout of 7.97 per cent, or 213,878 voters, as of 9am.
The voting process is proceeding smoothly, with voters continuing to arrive at polling centres across Johor to fulfil their civic responsibility.

Title: No ban on shorts at polling stations, says EC after voter allegedly stopped in Skudai
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — The Election Commission (EC) has clarified that there is no official dress code preventing voters from wearing shorts to polling stations, after an alleged incident involving a voter at a Skudai polling centre.
EC deputy chairman Azmi Sharom told
Malaysiakini
that the only restriction imposed was on clothing displaying political party logos or candidate branding.
“No such dress code. The only restriction is clothes with party logo or candidate,” he was quoted as saying.
The clarification came after a voter was allegedly barred from entering the polling station for the Skudai state seat at SMK Taman Tun Aminah for wearing shorts.
Photos circulating on Facebook showed a man in a yellow T-shirt and above-the-knee khaki shorts standing outside the school, with a caption claiming he was not allowed to enter the polling centre to vote.
The images also showed the man speaking to a school security guard and a sign at the school gate listing a visitor dress code that prohibits male visitors from wearing shorts, ripped jeans, sleeveless tops and slippers.
It was unclear whether the Facebook poster was the individual involved in the incident.
Apart from schools, voting for the Johor state election is also being conducted at public halls which do not impose visitor dress codes.

Title: Teo says MCMC received 29 complaints over online content during Johor campaign
Article:
KULAI, July 11 — The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) received 29 complaints related to online content involving fake news, hate speech and scams during the campaign period for the 16th Johor state election.
Deputy Communications Minister Teo Nie Ching said 17 of the complaints were related to the dissemination of fake news, while 11 involved hate speech and one concerned a fake account and impersonation.
“Nine hate speech cases involved racial issues, while one case each related to religion and the monarchy, categorised as 3R (race, religion and royalty) content,” she told reporters after casting her ballot at SJK © Kulai Besar here today.
Teo urged the public, especially voters, to be more vigilant against the spread of unverified information and not be easily misled by fake news or statements containing hate speech.
“Be digitally literate netizens and voters so that we can exercise our voting rights wisely,” she said.
Meanwhile, Teo, who is also the Kulai Member of Parliament, expressed her appreciation to all election personnel for ensuring the smooth conduct of the Johor state election polling process.
The 16th Johor state election sees 172 candidates contesting 56 State Legislative Assembly seats, with more than 2.6 million registered voters deciding the outcome today.
For the latest updates on the 16th Johor state election, visit https://prn.bernama.com/johor. — Bernama

Title: Penang governor hopes federal govt backs plan for Ceremonial Hall at Seri Mutiara
Article:
GEORGE TOWN, July 11 — Penang Yang Dipertua Negeri Tun Ramli Ngah Talib has expressed hope that the federal government will give due consideration to a proposal to build a Ceremonial Hall within the Seri Mutiara grounds to enhance the conduct of official state ceremonies and uphold the dignity of the institution of the Head of State.
He said such a facility is essential to ensure that investiture ceremonies for Penang’s state orders, decorations and medals are conducted with the appropriate decorum, tradition and grandeur.
Tun Ramli noted that it is common practice among the Malay Rulers and states headed by a Yang Dipertua Negeri for investiture ceremonies to be held in a Ceremonial Hall located within the palace or official residence of the Head of State, serving as a symbol of sovereignty, the continuity of tradition and the prestige of the institution.
“It is therefore fitting for Penang to also have a Ceremonial Hall that stands as a landmark of the state’s grandeur and a venue for preserving customs and heritage passed down through generations. I understand that the proposal for its construction has already been submitted to the federal government.
“I hope that the process proceeds smoothly and receives due consideration,” he said in his address at the 2026 Penang State Investiture Ceremony, held in conjunction with his 85th birthday celebrations, at Dewan Sri Pinang today.
The governor said the proposed Ceremonial Hall would enable the state government to better fulfil its responsibility to uphold the dignity, honour and sovereignty of Penang.
He also called on all parties to continue honouring the trust placed in them, strengthening unity and practising governance with integrity to ensure the state’s continued progress and prosperity amid growing global challenges.
He said the values of trustworthiness, integrity and accountability must guide leadership and public administration at every level, particularly as the world faces economic uncertainty, climate change, rapid technological advancement and an increasingly complex geopolitical environment.
“Every achievement must be grounded in trustworthiness, integrity and the confidence of the people. Indeed, the trust of the people is the cornerstone of a credible administration,” he said.
Tun Ramli added that state development policies must strike a balance between economic growth, environmental sustainability and social well-being to ensure their benefits are shared by all segments of society.
He also expressed confidence in the Penang government’s commitment to further strengthening the economy, advancing digital transformation and enhancing the state’s competitiveness as a trusted global investment and technology hub.
In addition, he urged the state government to continue fostering a culture of learning, openness and continuous improvement by adopting best practices from successful cities and countries to ensure Penang remains relevant and competitive.
Earlier, the investiture ceremony began with a 17-gun salute, followed by a royal salute, an inspection of the parade and a guard of honour.
Tun Ramli inspected the main guard of honour mounted by the Penang Federal Reserve Unit, comprising three officers and 103 personnel led by Major Hibatul Hakimi Mahamud.
Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil headed the list of 230 recipients honoured today after being conferred the Darjah Panglima Pangkuan Negeri (DPPN), which carries the title Datuk Seri.
A total of 101 individuals will receive state orders this year, while another 1,120 recipients will be conferred state decorations and medals during the four-day investiture ceremony, which continues on July 13, 14 and 15. — Bernama

Title: Johor polls see 7.97pc turnout by 9am, surpassing previous election pace
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, July 11— A total of 213,878 voters, or 7.97 per cent of the more than 2.6 million voters, had cast their ballots as of 9 am in the 16th Johor state election today.
The Election Commission (EC), in a Facebook post, said the Mahkota state constituency recorded the highest voter turnout at 15.8 per cent, followed by Tanjung Surat (14.7 per cent) and Pasir Raja (13.6 per cent).
Meanwhile, Bentayan and Simpang Jeram recorded the lowest voter turnout, with both registering 7.7 per cent, while Kukup recorded 7.6 per cent.
